title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: An announcement for the 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society in Glasgow, UK, listing Fabio Giglietto as an expert roundtable panelist discussing the challenges of public-interest research and the transition from CrowdTangle to the Meta Content Library.
description: An announcement for the #SMSociety conference panel featuring Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert speaker discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: An event announcement naming Fabio Giglietto as a panelist for the International Conference on Social Media & Society (SMSociety) in Glasgow. He was selected to present at the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable' exploring research infrastructure and data access for the public interest.
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Local news coverage highlighting how a scientific study by Professor Fabio Giglietto ('A Pretty Blunt Approach') on Meta's political content filters has entered the broader political sphere, serving as the basis for formal inquiries within the European Parliament regarding algorithmic interference in democracy.
description: An article detailing how a scientific study by Professor Fabio Giglietto concerning Meta's political content filters has influenced discussions and formal inquiries within the European Parliament.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: A news report summarizing Giglietto's computational social science study on Meta's algorithm. The article details how political groups in Italy (PD and FdI) are moving toward bipartisan legislation on digital platform regulation as a result of these findings.
description: A news article detailing a study by Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino on how Facebook's algorithm deprioritizes official politics and favors anti-European extremists, prompting Italian political parties to propose bipartisan digital platform regulation.
